reason: Both correctly diagnose the Goodhart dynamic (MTTR as mean hides distribution, incident reclassification/severity shopping, root-cause-vs-mitigation gap) and converge on similar corrective metrics (frequency, total impact-minutes, p90/p95, SLO/error-budget burn, pager fatigue proxies). Core substance is comparable. B includes two genuinely new, correct, decision-relevant points A lacks: (1) that MTTR is statistically noisy with typical quarterly incident volumes (citing the VOID report), meaning a 40% swing could partly be sampling variance; and (2) a qualitative on-call pulse survey plus change failure rate as an additional check. A is more polished in formatting but adds nothing substantive beyond B's coverage. Since correctness and decision-relevant usefulness matter more than presentation, B has a slight edge.
